research.product: Splunk HTTP Event Collector (Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform)
research.interface: HEC REST endpoints /services/collector/{event,raw,ack,health} with indexer acknowledgment (current Splunk Enterprise 9.x/10.x docs)

official_sources.title: About HTTP Event Collector Indexer Acknowledgment | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: the /services/collector/ack endpoint; channel required on every send and ack query (X-Splunk-Request-Channel header or channel query parameter, GUID format); ack query body {"acks":[...]}; ack response {"acks":{"0":true,...}}; HEC deletes an ackId's status after reporting true so re-querying returns false; poll-at-interval and resend-if-unacked guidance

official_sources.title: Format events for HTTP Event Collector | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: envelope keys event/time/host/source/sourcetype/index/fields; epoch <sec>.<ms> time format; batching by concatenated/newline-separated envelopes; fields must be flat (no nested objects) and are only indexed via the /event endpoint; raw endpoint requires a channel identifier

official_sources.title: HTTP Event Collector REST API endpoints | Splunk Enterprise
Troubleshoot HTTP Event Collector | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: the HEC response code table: 0 Success/200, 6 Invalid data format/400, 9 Server is busy/503, 10 Data channel is missing/400, 14 ACK is disabled/400, 17 HEC is healthy/200, 18 HEC is unhealthy queues are full/503, 26 HEC queue is at capacity/429; guidance that clients must act on response codes

official_sources.title: splunk/fluent-plugin-splunk-hec out_splunk_hec.rb (official Splunk HEC connector source)
official_sources.used_for: the gzip option: official Splunk HEC connector sets Content-Encoding: gzip and gzip-compresses the POST body when gzip_compression is enabled

official_sources.title: splunk/kafka-connect-splunk PostResponse.java (official Splunk HEC client source)
official_sources.used_for: the exact success wire response {"text":"Success","code":0,"ackId":<int>} — in particular the ackId key casing on the wire

research.language_rationale: Our shipping-floor forwarders are Python daemons. Splunk documents HEC as a raw REST interface (the official splunk-sdk-python does not cover HEC indexer acknowledgment), so stdlib urllib against the documented /services/collector endpoints is the credible, dependency-free integration surface; Splunk's own HEC connectors implement the same wire contract.

verified_facts: HEC auth is the Authorization: Splunk <token> header; tokens never belong in URLs.
With indexer acknowledgment enabled, every data send and ack query carries a channel: the X-Splunk-Request-Channel header or the channel query parameter; the value must be GUID-shaped.

Event envelopes allow exactly event, time, host, source, sourcetype, index and fields; time is epoch seconds with millisecond precision; batched envelopes are concatenated JSON objects (newline separation is legal).
fields must be a flat object (scalars or arrays of scalars) and is only indexed when sent to the /event endpoint.

A send with acknowledgment enabled answers {"text":"Success","code":0,"ackId":<int>}; the ack query POSTs {"acks":[ids]} to /services/collector/ack and gets back {"acks":{"<id>":bool}} with string keys.

The current help.splunk.com indexer-acknowledgment page's response table writes the key as 'ackID', but Splunk's own HEC client sources (kafka-connect-splunk PostResponse.java) parse the wire key 'ackId' with a comment showing the real response; the fixture pins the wire casing ackId.
Once HEC reports true for an ackId it deletes the status; re-querying that ackId returns false forever, so pollers must drop acked ids from later queries.

HEC status codes pinned from the official troubleshooting table: 0/200 Success, 6/400 Invalid data format, 9/503 Server is busy, 10/400 Data channel is missing, 14/400 ACK is disabled, 17/200 HEC is healthy, 18/503 queues full, 26/429 queue at capacity.
Splunk's official HEC connector gzips POST bodies and labels them Content-Encoding: gzip when compression is enabled.

Because acknowledgment provides delivery confirmation, resending an identical batch after an ambiguous transport failure is the documented-safe (at-least-once) recovery; unacked batches are resent after a timeout.
